publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: 43 Miracles of Mary
msItem.note: There are supplications at the beginning and the end of each miracle. The name of the initial supplicant has been erased in the vast majority of supplications. The new one, written over in a different red ink, is . The name of the original supplicant is , and his name appears on for the first time and is preserved in the rest of supplications until the end of the manuscript.

incipit.q: ስምዕ፡ አበውየ፡ ወአኅውየ፡ ወአኃትየ፡ እለ፡ ሀሎ፡ ክሙ፡ ውስተ፡ ዛቲ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስትያን፡ እንዘ፡ ትሴፈው፡ ከመ፡ ትሰምዑ፡ ትአምሪሃ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ቅድስት፡ ድንግል፡ በማርያም፡ ወላዲተ፡ አምላክ፡ ረዱ፡ እምቅዋም፡ ልዑል፡

support.note: The hole in has been sewn up.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation is provided by the library and is marked in pencil on the upper right edge of the recto folia.
supportDesc.condition: Despite some brownish stains (e.g., on ) and the general dirtiness of the parchment, the text is fully readable and both the parchment and the cover are in a very good condition. The headband is slightly damaged.

layout.ab: The upper line is written above the ruling.
The bottom line is written above the ruling.

handNote.seg: Divine names, incipits, punctuation marks, the words ሰላም፡ and ተፈስሒ፡ as well as Ethiopic numerals.
Divine names, incipits, punctuation marks, Ethiopic numerals and the supplicant’s name.

decoNote.desc: Lines of black and red dots at the end of almost each text part, e.g., on .

A miniature of three episodes of the miracle of Daqsǝyos, depicting , and an (
2).

A miniature depicting healing the priest, who has hurt his back while bowing before Her (
11).

A miniature depicting speaking to a deceased priest, while two angels are waiting to take his soul to Paradise (
12).

A miniature depicting one episode of the miracle of the the scribe Damianus and portraying him writing the name of with gold and silver inks (
14).

A miniature depicting one episode of the miracle of the scribe Damianus and portraying how appeared to Damianus on his death bed (
15) .

A miniature depicting one episode of the miracle of the Bishop of Rome ʾAbbās and portraying him touching the face of a woman during the Holy Mysteries (
18) .

A miniature depicting two episodes of the miracle of the Bishop of Rome ʾAbbās and portraying forgiving him and rejoining his hand (
19).

A miniature depicting Yǝsḥaq prostrating before (
21) .

A miniature of the miracle of Māryām of Dafrā depicting men going to a church on the day of the Nativity and leaving a child called Mary at home anf then receiving her soul after her death three days later (
22).

A miniature of the (
24) .

A miniature depicting Zacharias praying to (
28) .

A miniature depicting one episode of the miracle of a sick man and portraying bringing him to the river Jordan to baptize him there (
27).

A miniature of the miracle of the pregnant abbess Sophia, depicting commanding to two angels to help Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel, to deliver a child and to show it to the man called Felix (
76).

A miniature consisting of three episodes of the miracle of the pregnant abbess Sophia. In the first of them, Sophia stands before the Bishop, and in another one, Felix with her child is depicted before the Bishop. In the third one, the Bishop gives order concerning the education of the child. (
78).

A miniature consisting of three episodes of the miracle of Bārok. In the first of them, Bārok hosts a celebration in honour of . In the second of them, he is beaten by his foes. In the last one, he dies, and accompanied by two angels receives his soul (
81).

A miniature consisting of one episode of the miracle of the deacon ʾAnǝsṭāsyos and depicting consoling sick on his death bed (
83).

A miniature of the miracle of the monk of Dabra Qalǝmon who did not fast, depicting monks beating their brethren (
85).

A miniature of the the miracle of the monk of Dabra Qalǝmon who did not fast, depicting , a praying monk and two angels (
86).

A miniature consisting of three episodes of the miracle of the cannibal of Qǝmǝr and depicting the cannibal eating human flesh, giving his bow and arrows to farmers in exchange for two oxes, and giving water from his bottle to a leprous boy (
88).

A miniature of the miracle of the cannibal of Qǝmǝr, depicting Our Lord Jesus Christ in glory, protecting the cannibal’s soul from being carried off to hell by a , and an archangel weighing water and souls (
56).

A miniature depicting the story of and his rescue (
57).

A miniature representing the miracle of the rich man from Qʷalāsis and depicting healing the Greek merchant who had got an arrow in his eye (
48).

A miniature depicting a blind girl and her mother praying to , and two angels (
50).

A miniature depicting restoring the sight of the blind girl with her breast milk (
52).
A miniature consisting of three episodes and depicting the .

A miniature consisting of one episode of the miracle of two brothers who were scribes and depicting them falling into sin while copying the Book of the Miracles of (
56).

A miniature consisting of one episode of the miracle of two brothers who were scribes and depicting delivering their souls to two demons (
57).

A miniature consisting of two episodes of the miracle of Giyorgis Ḥaddis and depicting under the guise of a dove rescuing from the prison (
58).

A miniature of one episode of the miracle of the priest Kaṭir, depicting a church guard beating him (
60).

A miniature consisting of two episodes of the miracleof the priest Kaṭir and depicting answering to his prayers and an archangel punishing the church guard with paralysis (
61).

A miniature consisting of three episodes of the miracle of the two brothers from Dalgā and depicting the story of Nazib’s imprisonment (
64).

A miniature consisting of four episodes of the miracle of the two brothers from Dalgā and depicting a story of Nazib’s release by after his brother’s prayers to (
66).

A miniature depicting a formely lame person telling his healing story to the Archbishop so that the later stops being angry with another lame man praying for healing before the icon of (
68).

A miniature consisting of two episodes of the miracle of the leprous Bishop Mercurius and depicting the archbishop Zacharias prohibiting Bishop Mercurius from the ministering at the altar due to his illnes and healing the leprous bishop Mercurius (
70).

A miniature depicting either Zacharias or Mercurius administering the Holy Mysteries (
71).

A miniature consisting of two episodes of the miracle of the woman with a broken foot and depicting her praying in the church and healing this woman afterwards (
65).

A miniature of the miracle of Zakāryās, depicting the robbers becoming monks after having witnessed Zakāryās speak to (
31).

A miniature illustrating one episode from the miracle of Yolyānā and Barbārā and depicting them and two thieves, who stole the bread from them (
33).

A miniature illustrating one episode from the miracle of Yolyānā and Barbārā and depicting Yolyānā, Barbārā, and two thieves standing before and two angels (
34).

A miniature depicting the story of three men suffering a shipwreck, two of whom were rescued by , and the third one being punished for mocking Her (
36).

A miniature depicting two Arabs making offerings to a Monastery (
37).

A miniature depicting appearing to a priest in his dream and ordering him to build a monastery in Her name (
39).

A miniature of one episode from the miracle of the church of ʿƎqonā, depicting it being transferred by the power of (
41).

A miniature consisting of two episodes of the miracle and depicting restoring the sight of a blind priest, who has been praying to her, with her breast milk (
44).

A miniature of an episode from the miracle of the rich man from Qʷalāsis depicting a Greek merchant getting wounded in his eye by an arrow (
46).

A miniature depicting the miracle of the pregnant woman who was caught by the tide. The woman is portrayed describing the miracle of being rescued by after having been abandoned by companion pilgrims during their journey to the shrine (
61).
A miniature depicting the miracle of a thirsty dog portraying giving a thirsty dog water from her shoe, surrounded by two angels.

A miniature depicting two companions of , one of whom is drawing water and another one is driving away a thirsty dog from the water jars (
64).

item.desc: The ownership note is written in pencil first and then outlined in black ink (№2, This manuscript is the property of Lady Meux).
ዘአትለውኪያ፡ ማርያም፡ is written by the same hand that added the name of to supplications.
The title, ተአምረ፡ ማርያም፡ ዘቅዱስ፡ መድኃኔ፡ ዓለም፡, is written in a brownish ink.
The space for a supplicant’s name was left blank.

binding.decoNote: Brown tooled leather cover with colourful textile inlays with a floral pattern. The blind tooled ornament includes crosses, double line fillets, double circles, and other ornamental elements.
Endbands are present, the headband is slightly damaged.

history.provenance:
The manuscript contains numerous supplications for and . The name of the latter has been added later by another hand in a different red colour. As it is evident from the ownership note, the manuscript belonged at some point to
Lady Valerie Susie Meux of Theobald's Park, Waltham Cross, Essex. After her death in 1910 the whole collection was sold. Many of the objects, including this manuscript, were bought by William Randolph Hearst, whose collection was sold at Sotheby's, 11–12 July 1939.

bibl.note: This is the manuscript used by Budge in his edition; the majority of the miniatures of the manuscript were also reprinted.

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

revisionDesc.change: Updated content, added physical description in accordance to the digital copy
Edited after the review of Carsten Hoffmann and Magdalena Krzyżanowska
